channel.title: Andy Zhu's blog
channel.description: Notes on cryptography, algorithms, math, and other things I find interesting.

item.title: The Discrete Fourier Transform
item.description: From continuous frequencies to roots of unity — how the DFT, FFT, and NTT turn O(n²) convolutions into O(n log n), with applications in competitive programming and the real world.

item.title: The elliptic curve on my homepage, demystified
item.description: What the animated curve behind every page is actually computing — and why the same operation secures half the internet.

item.title: FRI: fast low-degree testing by recursive folding
item.description: How a clever split of a polynomial into its even and odd parts lets a verifier check degree in logarithmic queries — the recursive trick behind modern STARKs.
